content: Self-supervised world model memory agents projected for 2025 can integrate a hierarchical curiosity-gated episodic memory buffer derived from neural architecture for episodic planning, where only unexpected, prediction-error-triggered episodes are encoded into long-term world model memory to avoid catastrophic forgetting and reduce active learning sample complexity. This gating mechanism repurposes the intrinsic reward signal from curiosity-driven reinforcement learning as a memory access control gate rather than just a reward bonus for exploration.
source_topics: neural architecture memory episodic planning agent
